gaps: statpack relist-count and CVSG cuts are unpopulated ((unknown) bucket only), yet relist count was the single most informative structured signal for this cell
fedcourts query has no free-text/topic match for cert dockets (citation filter returned nothing), so finding sibling petitions on the same legal question relied on scanning the general ranked list
supremecourt.gov blocks the web fetcher (HTTP 403), so granted-QP scope had to come from secondary coverage
a provisioned copy of the cert-stage reply and supplemental briefs would have helped; the docket shows them but only petition/BIO/QP were provisioned
helpful: corpus query rows carrying date_cert_granted/denied and distribution_count for sibling petitions — this revealed the June 30 companion grants and same-day denials, the decisive signal
statpack modern-cert base rate as the anchor
notes: Ranged corpus reads were heavy (~105 MB / 402 GETs per query invocation); a lighter index for docket-level metadata would cut cell cost. GVR-labels-as-granted (cert_signals) matters for calibrating held petitions; worth stating in the predict prompt so predictors map hold->GVR paths onto the granted side consistently.
